visual:
NEW data context: "Books Read This Month"
  - Data Table: Aisha: 20, Ben: 35, Carlos: 55, Dana: 80
  - Scale Preview System with "Scale of 5" pre-selected
  - Vertical graph currently shows axis 0-50, initially empty
guide: "Here's data about how many books four students read this month. Dana read the most—80 books!"
guide_2: [Think-Aloud] "Let me try Scale of 5 and see what happens."
animation: Preview generates. Aisha (20), Ben (35), Carlos (55) bars appear normally. Dana's bar (80) extends PAST the current graph boundary. ⚠️ indicator appears.
guide_3: "Hmm. Carlos's bar is just above my graph, but Dana's bar goes past the top. The graph only goes up to 50 right now."
guide_4: [Think-Aloud] "I COULD make the graph taller to fit 80. Let's see... I'd need 55, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80..."
animation_2: Axis extends upward, showing tick marks appearing: 55, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80. The axis now looks crowded with many tick marks.
guide_5: "That's a LOT of numbers on the axis. 0, 5, 10, 15, 20... all the way up to 80. That's 17 tick marks!"
visual_highlight: Axis shown with all 17 tick marks visible (0, 5, 10, 15... 75, 80)
guide_6: "It works... but is there a better way?"
key_teaching_point: "When one scale needs too many tick marks, try a bigger scale."
